question.label: Political conversations
question.name: Political conversations — what makes them hard for you to stay in?
question.description: Drills into the specific dynamic behind his retreat from influence-heavy situations.

answer: I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.

assessment.note: Hesitation around shaping cross-org alignment signals a core next-level growth gap; [THREAD-DEFERRED] cross-org alignment hesitation not drilled because drill cap forced stage advance.

new_queue.label: Stretch now
new_queue.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?

new_queue.label: Senior scope now
new_queue.name: Where does your work feel most like Head of Product work right now?

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Role definition
new_queue.name: What does the next-level Head of Product role mean to you?

new_queue.label: Delegate more
new_queue.name: What are you still holding that someone else could own?

new_queue.label: Next experiment
new_queue.name: What's one concrete shift you want to try before our next conversation?

history.answer_excerpt: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
